title: How Ramp engineers accelerate code review with Codex
optimized_headline: Ramp Engineers Speed Up Code Reviews Using Codex: Here's How
raw_excerpt: How Ramp engineers use Codex with GPT-5.5 to review code and ship improvements, allowing them to get substantive feedback in minutes instead of hours.
raw_body: How Ramp engineers use Codex with GPT-5.5 to review code and ship improvements, allowing them to get substantive feedback in minutes instead of hours.
speedrun: Ramp engineers are utilizing Codex powered by GPT-5.5 to enhance their code review process. This approach enables them to receive substantial feedback in mere minutes, significantly reducing the time it takes to ship improvements. By streamlining code reviews, they can iterate faster and improve their software more efficiently. This matters now as it highlights a shift towards faster development cycles in tech.
why_it_matters: Engineers can now get quicker feedback, which could lead to faster iterations and improved product quality.
This reflects a broader trend in the tech industry towards leveraging AI for efficiency, potentially reshaping development workflows.

lenses.eli12: Ramp engineers are using a smart tool called Codex to speed up how they check their code. Instead of waiting hours for feedback, they can get it in just minutes. Think of it like having a helpful assistant who gives you quick advice on your homework. This matters because faster feedback can help teams build better software more quickly.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, using Codex means faster code reviews, which could lead to quicker product launches. This efficiency can reduce costs associated with lengthy development cycles. A practical implication is that teams can focus on innovation rather than getting bogged down in the review process.
lenses.engineer: From a technical perspective, Ramp engineers are leveraging Codex with GPT-5.5, which enhances their code review efficiency. By reducing feedback time from hours to minutes, they can address issues and implement improvements much more rapidly. This approach underscores the potential of AI models to optimize development processes, although specific benchmarks for performance improvements weren't detailed.
